carbon_policy_signal.item: ETS review due July; Poland criticizes 'insane' EU climate policy framework; watch for floor/ceiling and scope tweaks affecting EUA trading ranges and power-carbon spark.
carbon_policy_signal.why_it_matters: July Commission ETS reset will reshape carbon price signal and power-generation merit order; Polish political pushback signals possible supply-side adjustments affecting EUA volatility and thermal-baseload economics.

risk_flags: EU storage at 40.76% (17th percentile, 14pp deficit) with only 4 weeks to peak refill; any demand surge or LNG diversion to US tightens summer margin.
GB Power at 125.28 EUR/MWh (90th percentile) while DE Power at 119.1 (68th percentile); GB premium signals outage risk or interconnect constraint.
ETS review due July; Polish political opposition to carbon framework creates downside risk to EUA floor/ceiling and power-carbon spark volatility.
June 16 EU trade vote on Trump accord could trigger tariff escalation (10% forced-labor levy proposed), disrupting LNG capex and transatlantic energy investment sentiment.

base.summary: GB tightness persists; storage deficit slowly tightens through June. TTF and DE Power stable around current levels.

downside.summary: Trade accord vote defers tariff escalation; EU-US tensions ease. El Niño forecasts weaken; cooler weather reduces AC load.

upside.summary: El Niño heat intensifies; European hydropower disappoints; US LNG export tightness diverts fewer cargoes. Cooling demand surge.

themes: Summer heat and cooling demand upside
ETS review volatility, tariff escalation risk
Renewables at seasonal median, insufficient offset
top_drivers: GB Power at 90th percentile amid tight summer supply; storage deficit (14pp below seasonal) limits thermal margin headroom.
El Niño heat warning drives cooling-demand upside and hydropower risk; cascades into gas call and power price support through summer curve.
ETS review (July) + Polish political opposition + June 16 trade tariff vote create policy and sentiment volatility; no immediate structural repricing yet.
top_takeaway: GB Power at 90th percentile; EU storage 14pp below seasonal norm signals tight thermal dispatch into summer amid El Niño heat risk.

watchlist.why: 90th percentile vs 68th suggests localized supply tightness or interconnect binding; any UK nuclear/wind outage could spike GB differentially.

watchlist.metric: EU storage refill pace to Aug
watchlist.why: At 40.76% (17th pctile), summer margin is thin; any sustained LNG diversion to US (record 2027 expected) or geopolitical supply loss extends deficit.
July ETS review and Polish pushback create bidirectional volatility; watch for floor/ceiling proposals that could reprice power-carbon merit order.

watchlist.metric: El Niño heat intensity & hydropower forecasts
watchlist.why: UN warning of intensifying heat drives cooling-demand upside and hydropower generation risk; cascades into gas call and power price support.

watchlist.metric: US LNG export parity to TTF
watchlist.why: US gas demand near highs (EIA); tighter Henry Hub margins reduce cargo diversion probability but increase TTF premium if LNG becomes scarce.

watchlist_dated.name: EU trade accord final vote (June 16)
watchlist_dated.why: Tariff escalation enactment risk; 10% forced-labor levy could disrupt LNG capex sentiment and Cal+1 curve volatility.

watchlist_dated.name: July ETS review publication
watchlist_dated.why: Carbon supply/policy reset; watch for floor/ceiling tweaks affecting EUA pricing and power-carbon spark around merit-order threshold.
